product: SAP S/4HANA Cloud — Business Partner (A2X) OData V2 API

service.base_path: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER — appended to the system host; entity sets such as A_BusinessPartner and A_BusinessPartnerAddress hang off it
service.protocol: OData 2.0; JSON is requested with $format=json and Accept: application/json
service.authentication: communication-user basic auth in this fixture: Authorization: Basic base64(user:password) on EVERY request, including followed __next links and deferred navigation URIs

query_serialization.filter: $filter=<expr> with eq comparisons joined by ' and ' in call order; string literals single-quoted; spaces percent-encoded as %20
query_serialization.quote_escaping: a single quote inside an OData V2 string literal is escaped by doubling it: O'Hara -> 'O''Hara'
query_serialization.expand: $expand=to_BusinessPartnerAddress[,path/deeper] — comma-separated navigation paths, slash for depth
query_serialization.inlinecount: $inlinecount=allpages adds a decimal string __count to the d envelope

response_envelope.server_paging: a __next link identifies the next partial set and MUST be requested verbatim — the mock plants a srvMarker query parameter that a rebuilt URL loses
response_envelope.expanded_navigation: an expanded collection-valued navigation property arrives inline as { "results": [ ...entities... ] }
response_envelope.deferred_navigation: an unexpanded navigation property arrives as { "__deferred": { "uri": "<absolute url>" } }; the uri is fetched as-is and answers a normal d.results feed
response_envelope.datetime: Edm.DateTime serializes as /Date(<ms since epoch>)/ and decodes to a UTC instant

error_document.rule: non-2xx responses raise an exception exposing httpStatus, error.code, message.value; the whole error object — including every SAP innererror member — is preserved verbatim and navigable
error_document.redaction: credentials and the Authorization header value never appear in exception text

mock_fixture.system_origin: loopback http://127.0.0.1:{port} standing in for the S/4HANA host
